svg.title: Issue — one fabricated answer about cover is a regulatory event
svg.desc: A fabricated answer that a customer is covered abroad, when the policy says otherwise, triggers a harm chain: the customer relies on it, the claim is refused, the firm may be bound to honour it or pay redress via the Financial Ombudsman, and at scale it is systemic rather than a one-off. It may breach FCA Consumer Duty (communications must be clear, fair and not misleading — the FCA confirms this covers chatbot output), FCA insurance conduct rules (ICOBS), the UK GDPR accuracy principle where it states something false about the customer's own cover, and contract/misrepresentation law. The FCA is technology-neutral: AI answers get no exemption.
svg.text: Issue
One fabricated sentence about cover isn't a bad answer — it's a regulatory event.
THE HARM CHAIN
"Yes — your policy covers you while abroad."
…the policy wording says it does not. Fluent, confident, and fabricated.
The customer relies on it
books travel, or makes a claim, assuming they're covered
The claim is refused
financial loss, distress, and a formal complaint
The firm may be on the hook
bound to honour what it said, or pay redress (Financial Ombudsman)
At scale, it's systemic
not one bad answer — thousands a day, the same silent flaw
The answer is effectively a statement about the customer's contract.
Get it wrong and you haven't disappointed a customer — you've mis-stated cover,
created liability, and produced a compliance breach — invisibly, at volume.
WHAT IT MAY VIOLATE
FCA Consumer Duty (PRIN 2A)
Communications must be clear, fair & not misleading —
the FCA confirms this covers chatbot output directly.
FCA insurance conduct (ICOBS)
A customer must not be misled about their cover.
UK GDPR accuracy (Art. 5(1)(d), Art. 16)
A false statement about the customer's own cover is
inaccurate personal data — must be accurate, rectified.
Contract law / misrepresentation
The firm may be legally bound by what it told the customer.
The control that answers all of these
Standard GS2B1-01 — output grounding gate
AWS Generative AI Lens · GENSEC02-BP01
The FCA is technology-neutral — no AI-specific rulebook.
A chatbot's words are the firm's words; existing rules apply in full.
Regulatory positions verified against FCA & ICO guidance, 2026 — not legal advice.
